name: Fudge Chip Poundcake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Butter and flour a 9 x 5-inch loaf pan.
3. In a heavy small saucepan, blend together sugar and cocoa. Stir in water.
4. Bring mixture to a simmer over medium-low heat, stirring until smooth.
5. Simmer gently for three minutes. Allow the mixture to cool completely.
6. In a large bowl of electric mixer, cream 1/2 cup butter until fluffy.
7. Gradually beat in sugar, then eggs one at a time. Blend in vanilla.
8. Sift flour with baking powder and salt. Add to batter alternately with milk, blending well after each addition. Fold in chocolate chips.
9. Turn one-third of the batter into the prepared pan, spreading higher along the edges.
10. Divide remaining batter in half. Blend half into the cooled cocoa mixture with baking soda.
11. Spoon over the blended batter into the pan and spread, leaving a 1/2-inch border at the edges.
12. Top with the remaining light batter. Bake until a tester inserted in the center comes out clean, about 65 to 70 minutes. Cool in the pan for 10 minutes. Invert the cake onto a rack and cool completely.
